task:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. User showed screenshots of DBeaver/DBGate showing analytics.duckdb with tables (chat_questions, mcp_ai_query_log, mcp_query_log) - chat_questions had data but mcp_ai_query_log appeared empty.

2. User said the analytics.duckdb was copied from production server. I explained data was in the WAL file.

3. I copied both .duckdb and .wal files from production. User reopened and saw mcp_ai_query_log still empty but chat_questions was missing.

4. Investigation revealed THREE analytics.duckdb files on server:
   - `/root/safecast-mcp-server/analytics.duckdb` - MCP server (has mcp_* logs)
   - `/analytics.duckdb` - unified-server at filesystem root (has chat_questions)
   - `/root/safecast-map-MCP/analytics.duckdb` - old archived repo

5. User asked to consolidate into one file and document it.

6. I entered plan mode. Key constraint: DuckDB only supports one writer process, so two separate services can't share one .duckdb file.

7. I proposed moving chat_questions to PostgreSQL (recommended). User asked about DuckLake instead.

8. I researched DuckLake - it uses PostgreSQL as catalog with Parquet data files, enabling multi-process concurrent writes. But DuckLake requires DuckDB v1.3.0+ and current setup uses v1.2.1 (via marcboeker/go-duckdb v1.8.5).

9. User said to switch to the new duckdb-go driver. I found `duckdb/duckdb-go/v2` bundles DuckDB v1.5.0.

10. I initially started implementing PostgreSQL migration approach (created migration, modified chat_logging.go, removed DuckDB files). User interrupted asking about DuckLake with the new driver.

11. I reverted all PostgreSQL migration changes via git checkout.

12. Switched driver: replaced `marcboeker/go-duckdb` with `duckdb/duckdb-go/v2` in 3 files.

13. Updated DuckDB CLI on production from v1.2.1 to v1.5.

I just eneter a question in the widget and got an answer.  If I run the follwoing command I will get the latest  analytics.duckdb on my laptop?

 ssh -i ~/.ssh/safecast-deploy root@65.108.24.131 "systemctl stop safecast-new-map && /usr/local/bin/duckdb /analytics.duckdb -c 'CHECKPOINT;' && systemctl start safecast-new-map" && scp -i ~/.ssh/safecast-deploy root@65.108.24.131:/analytics.duckdb ~/Documents/Safecast/analytics.duckdb
